en.title: 49 Malawians return home after Zimbabwe bus crash
en.body: Forty-nine Malawians who lived through a bus crash in Zimbabwe have come back home. They crossed into Malawi on Saturday through Mwanza Border. The group has 27 men, 16 women, and six children. They were on a bus that went from South Africa to Malawi. On May 5, the bus turned over between Kotwa Town and Nyamapanda. About 60 people were on the bus. Seventeen Malawians died in the crash.
en.body_more:
When the survivors came to Malawi, doctors at Mwanza District Hospital checked them all. Four people were very ill and went to Queen Elizabeth Central Hospital for more help. The other 44 people stayed at Kamuzu Stadium in Blantyre.

Wilson Moleni, a government leader from the Disaster Management office, met the group. He said the government worked with Zimbabwe to bring them home. He said they will check each person to see who needs more help from doctors and who can go home. Moleni said thank you to Zimbabwe for helping during this hard time.

One survivor, Enock Sisya from Nkhatabay, told people what happened. He said the government helped them from the day of the crash. He said doctors helped them in Zimbabwe. They got food and rides and everything they wanted. Sisya said he is happy the government did this.

The 17 people who died in the crash are coming back to Malawi on Monday, May 11. Their families are waiting for them.

ny.title: Anthu 49 a Malawi abwera nyumba atengeka potengeka
ny.body: Anthu 49 ochokera Malawi akupilira pansi pa bumotu bwa basibasi ku Zimbabwe abwera nyumba. Anafika Malawi Sabata la May 9 kudzera m'Mwanza Border. Gulu ili lili ndi amuna 27, akazi 16, ndi ana 6. Iwo anali pabasi lolowera ku Malawi kuchokera ku South Africa. Pa May 5, basibasi linu linagweduka pakati pa Kotwa Town ndi Nyamapanda. Anthu pafupifupi 60 anali pabasi. Anthu 17 achokera Malawi anafa.
ny.body_more:
Anthu ankafika Malawi, akatembenuza mlumbi m'Mwanza District Hospital. Anthu 4 anali okhuta kwambiri ndipo anatumizidwa ku Queen Elizabeth Central Hospital (QECH) kuti apatsidwe thandizo lamankhwala. Anthu 44 ena anatengezedwa ku Kamuzu Stadium ku Blantyre.

Wilson Moleni, wina wa anthu akutsatira zimangazi, anawakumana ndi iwo. Anati boma lilizogwiranso ntchito ndi Zimbabwe kuti tizileme awa abwere nyumba. Anati iwo azayang'ana munthu aliyense kuti aziwona kuti ndani akufuna mankhwala ndi ndani angafike nyumba. Moleni anatsimikiza Zimbabwe kuti idathandiza pano pantchito yayikulu.

Wina wa anthu ankate mulingo, Enock Sisya wobwera Nkhatabay, anauza zomwe zidachitika. Anati boma lidathandiza kuchokera lililini lija. Anati kuchipatala ku Zimbabwe, adapatsidwa chilichonse amamveka. Analowa mwa luso ndi mayendedwe ndi chilichonse. Sisya anati ali mwacholera kuti boma linachita izi.

Anthu 17 ankufa mu bumotu ino akubwera Malawi Lolemba, May 11. Mamilandu awo akusumikirira.

en.title: Big derby match: Wanderers and Bullets clash tonight
en.body: Mighty Wanderers and FCB Nyasa Bullets play tonight in Lilongwe. The match is the Blantyre derby, the biggest football game in Malawi. It starts at Bingu National Stadium under lights. Wanderers have won four games in a row against Bullets. Bullets want to stop this. The two teams also played one month ago. Wanderers won that match 1-0.
en.body_more:
Bullets have lost their last four derby games. Coach Gilbert Chirwa said his team is ready to fight back. He said the boys are working hard and playing well. In the first two games this season, Bullets drew 2-2 with Civil Service and won 1-0 against Mafco. Chirwa said they made many chances but did not score enough goals. Now they want to do better. He said: "We are not giving up. If you look back, Wanderers came from nowhere and started winning. Now we want to change the result."

Wanderers have one big player: Sama Thierry Tanjong. He is a forward from Cameroon. He scored in all four derby wins. Bullets captain Blessing Mpokera must watch him. But Wanderers also have other good players like Gaddie Chirwa and Promise Kamwendo. Bullets have new players too. Clever Kalambo and George Chaomba scored in their first games. Another player, Yamikani Mologeni, is a good passer who can help the forwards.

Wanderers assistant coach McDonald Mtetemera is in charge tonight because the head coach is away. He said the team is ready. He said: "We have the depth in our squad. Our fans will watch beautiful football." Last season, Wanderers beat Bullets 1-0 and 3-0 in league games, and won 1-0 in the cup. The match will help Bullets show they can play better. The league said no glass bottles can be sold at the stadium, and no one can come in during the last minutes.

ny.title: Derby yachikulu: Wanderers ndi Bullets amalumikizana usiku
ny.body: Mighty Wanderers ndi FCB Nyasa Bullets amasewera usiku uno ku Lilongwe. Iyi ndi ndege yachikulu ya mpira mu Malawi. Amasewera pa Bingu National Stadium ndipo kudzakhala ndi kuwala. Wanderers awina masewera anu anayi mosalala ndi Bullets. Bullets akufuna kutseka izi. Timu ziwiri zimakumbukirana mwezi umodzi zapitali. Wanderers anawina 1-0.
ny.body_more:
Bullets asowa masewera anayi a derby mosalala. Mkozi Gilbert Chirwa anati timu yake ili yongoyendera. Anati abwino alizi akugwira ntchito. M'masewera awiri oyambirira msimu uno, Bullets anagawana 2-2 ndi Civil Service ndipo anawina 1-0 kutsutsana ndi Mafco. Chirwa anati anapanga zingilo zingapo koma sanathe kugona. Pano akufuna kugwira bwino. Anati: "Satsikani. Mukubuyerera kumbuyo, Wanderers ankabwera ndi kumayamba kuwina. Pano nife tifuna kusintha zinthu."

Wanderers ali ndi mngwazi umodzi wachikulu: Sama Thierry Tanjong. Ali mnjira woyambira wochoka ku Cameroon. Anagona masewera anayi onsewa a derby. Kapten wa Bullets Blessing Mpokera ayenera kumuyang'ana. Koma Wanderers alipo ndi anthu ena aabwino ngati Gaddie Chirwa ndi Promise Kamwendo. Bullets alipo ndi abwino atsopano. Clever Kalambo ndi George Chaomba anagona mu masewera awo oyamba. Yamikani Mologeni ndi njira yabwino yotumbulutsa mpira kwa mnjira. 

Mkozi wa Wanderers McDonald Mtetemera adzamvetsa usiku uno chifukwa mkozi wamkulu salipo. Anati timu yake ili yongoyendera. Anati: "Timu yathu ili yambiri. Anthu atsikana adzaona mpira wabwino." Msimu wapitali, Wanderers anawina 1-0 ndi 3-0 pa ndege ya ligi, ndipo anawina 1-0 pa mpikisano wa nsanja. Masewera ano adzathandiza Bullets kuonekeza kuti azingati kugwira bwino. Ligi linanena kuti botolo sazigulitsidwe ku bwalo, ndipo palibe mmene anthu angapite kumapeto kwa masewera.

en.title: Malawi loses to Nigeria, misses World Cup
en.body: Malawi's Under-20 women's football team lost their World Cup dream yesterday. They beat Nigeria 2-1 at Bingu National Stadium in Lilongwe. But Nigeria won the tie 3-2 on total goals. The match was the second leg of a qualifying game. Malawi had lost the first match 2-0 in Lagos one week before. This loss means Malawi will not go to the World Cup in Poland.
en.body_more:
Forward Faith Chinzimu scored twice for Malawi in yesterday's match. She was not able to play in the first game because of club work. In the tenth minute, she put the ball in the net from a free-kick by Olivia Phikani. The crowd was very happy. Nigeria almost scored back, but goalkeeper Emily Nkhwazi made a good save. She stopped Folajom Alaba Olabiyi one-on-one.

Chinzimu scored again in the fifty-five minute. She took the ball from a Nigeria player and ran from the middle of the field into the box. She shot and scored. But Nigeria came back strong. In the sixty-first minute, Precious Chimere Oscar scored for Nigeria. This goal ended Malawi's chance to go to the World Cup. Malawi had needed to win by three goals with no reply. Nigeria needed only a draw to go to Poland.

Malawi coach Maggie Chombo-Sadik said after the game that her team made a bad mistake. She said Nigeria's goal should not have gone in. Before the match, Chombo-Sadik said the team had hope because Chinzimu and Rose Kabzere were back. Both players were in other countries and could not play the first game. The coach said good support from home fans could help them win. Nigeria's coach Moses Adukwu said both teams played well.

Malawi had won other games to reach this round. They beat Guinea Bissau 6-2 on total goals. They also beat South Sudan 3-1 and knocked out Central African Republic 4-2. But Nigeria was too strong in this tie. The World Cup will be in September in Poland.

ny.title: Malawi akutaya khalidwe la World Cup
ny.body: Gulu la Malawi likuopeza za m'madzimbabwi lachipinga Champions U-20 linalakwitsa. Malawi inaoneka 2-1 kwa Nigeria pa Bingu National Stadium m'lilongwe kumapeto. Koma Nigeria inaoneka 3-2 pamodzi ndi gawo lachinthu. Imeneyi ndi masewera achachiŵiri. Malawi inamalawo 2-0 m'ngodya yoyamba kwa mwezi umodzi kale. Malawi sachitha m'ndlano yama World Cup mu Poland.
ny.body_more:
Woyimira Faith Chinzimu anathupi ziwiri za Malawi pa gawo lachiwiri. Anatatha gawo loyamba chifukwa a club. Mwaminiti ya 10, analemba songo kuchokera pa free-kick ya Olivia Phikani. Anthu a m'Stadium anasangalala kwambiri. Nigeria anayesa kuwonongeka, koma goalkeeper Emily Nkhwazi anachiika bwino. Anasunga Folajom Alaba Olabiyi pawokha kwa kwa gate.

Chinzimu anathupi gawo lachiwiri mwaminiti ya 55. Anadula mpira pa wamkulu wa Nigeria ndipo akamphasa kupita mu half line mpaka ku box. Analemba bwino. Koma Nigeria inabwera ndifu. Mwaminiti ya 61, Precious Chimere Oscar anathupi songo kwa Nigeria. Songo ili linakumalizira chiyembekezo cha Malawi kupita ku World Cup. Malawi inayenera kuoneka ndi migomero itatu popanda Malawi iwoneka songo. Nigeria inayenera kumvetsa wina aliyense kuti ipite ku Poland.

Wophunzitsa Malawi Maggie Chombo-Sadik anathanzi pambuyo pa masewera kuti gululo linalakwitsa. Anathanzi kuti songo ya Nigeria inayenera kusiya. Papita pa masewera, Chombo-Sadik anathanzi kuti gululo linalingaliranso chifukwa Chinzimu ndi Rose Kabzere anabwera. Ospolo onse akuti anthu onyamula mpira abwera kudziko linako ndipo sanathe kumasewera gawo loyamba. Wophunzitsa anathanzi kuti tilibe mantha ndipo anthu a kumnyumba atha kuthandiza. Wophunzitsa wa Nigeria Moses Adukwu anathanzi kuti gululo likuopeza bwino.

Malawi inakwera gawo ili pambuyo pa kuchita bwino m'masewera ena. Inaoneka Guinea Bissau 6-2 pamodzi ndi zinthu. Inaonenatu South Sudan 3-1 ndi Central African Republic 4-2. Koma Nigeria inali amphamvu kwambiri. World Cup idzayamba mu September mu Poland.

en.title: Felix Washon elected leader of Misa Malawi
en.body: Felix Washon, a communications expert, has been elected chairperson of Misa Malawi. Misa is the Media Institute of Southern Africa. Washon won the vote on Saturday in Lilongwe with 150 votes. He will lead for three years. He takes over from Golden Matonga. Three other people ran but got fewer votes.
en.body_more:
Innocent Mphongolo came second with 121 votes. Alex Banda got 78 votes and Janet Mtali got 55 votes. The National Initiative for Civic Education, called Nice Trust, counted the votes. They said the election was fair and honest.

Washon works for Red Cross Malawi. After the vote, he said thank you to all members. He said he will lead in an open and honest way. He also said thank you to people who voted for other people. He said the new team will build on the work of the old team. He said he will work with people he competed against.

Golden Matonga, the old leader, said well done to Washon. He asked the new leaders to make Misa stronger. He said they must help journalists in Malawi and help them live better. Nathan Majawa was elected vice chairperson with 370 votes. His rival, Luciano Milala, got only 20 votes. Dorothy Kachitsa was elected to the executive with 277 votes.

The voting took place in three main towns. Blantyre, Lilongwe, and Mzuzu each had a voting centre. Members in 14 districts voted online. Voting started at 7am and ended at 3pm, but was made one hour longer. Nice Trust said the election was free and fair.

ny.title: Felix Washon asankhidwa kukhala mtsogoleri wa Misa Malawi
ny.body: Felix Washon, wina amene amagwira ntchito ya komunikeyeshoni, wasankhidwa kukhala mtsogoleri wa Misa Malawi. Misa ndi bungwe la Media Institute of Southern Africa. Washon adapambana mfundo ndi mapota 150 pa Sabato mu Lilongwe. Adzasogolera kwa zaka zitatu. Iye amalowa malo a Golden Matonga. Anthu atatu ena adayeseza koma anapeza mapota ochepa.
ny.body_more:
Innocent Mphongolo anabwira wachiwiri ndi mapota 121. Alex Banda anapeza 78 mapota ndipo Janet Mtali anapeza 55 mapota. Bungwe la National Initiative for Civic Education, lomwe likuwoneka kuti Nice Trust, linawerenga mapota. Linati mpira inali yabwino komanso yonenepa.

Washon akugwira ntchito kwa Red Cross Malawi. Atakati akusankhidwa, anasiya mwano kwa mamembala onse. Anati adzasogolera moyamba mwambiri. Anasiya mwano kwa anthu amene anayesa anthu ena. Anati gulu latsopano ligamange ntchito ya gulu lakale. Anati adzagwira ntchito ndi anthu amene ankasantsa naye.

Golden Matonga, mtsogoleri wakale, anati bwino kwambiri kwa Washon. Anacoka guluyo kuti igulitse bungwe likhale lamphamvu. Anacoka kuti aloowe akalimbitsa zina za ajomalawi komanso kuwaponza anthu aja. Nathan Majawa asankhidwa kukhala mtsogoleri wachachiwiri ndi mapota 370. Mdani wake Luciano Milala adapeza mapota 20 okha. Dorothy Kachitsa asankhidwa kuti akhale mu bungwe lowina ndi mapota 277.

Kumvota kunachitika m'maguta atatu. Blantyre, Lilongwe, ndi Mzuzu analibe malo oyesa mapota. Mamembala mu distrikiti 14 anayesa mapota pa intaneti. Kumvota kunayamba pa 7 kumawa ndipo kunatha 3 pm, koma linatsika nthawi ya ola limodzi. Nice Trust linati mpira inali yabwino komanso yonenepa.

en.title: Eight die in bus crash as Malawi and South Africa talk safety
en.body: Eight people from Malawi died in a road crash in South Africa on Thursday. A bus left Malawi on Tuesday and crashed on the N1 Highway in Limpopo Province. The bus turned over near the Witvlag turn-off. Forty people were also hurt. At the same time, Malawi's foreign minister met South Africa's top diplomat in Lilongwe to ask for help and safety for Malawians.
en.body_more:
The bus that crashed was a Munorurama passenger bus. One person is still very ill. Twenty-two people got serious hurt. Seventeen others got small hurt. The government is trying to find out the names of all the Malawians on the bus. Malawi's diplomats in South Africa are helping the hurt people and will bring the bodies home.

This crash is not the only bad thing that happened. Another bus from South Africa turned over in Zimbabwe on Tuesday. That crash killed seventeen people and hurt forty-five. Those people who lived were brought back to Malawi. The government is bringing the dead bodies home from Zimbabwe now.

Malawi's foreign minister George Chaponda met South Africa's top diplomat Mavivi Myakayaka-Manzini on Friday. He asked South Africa to keep Malawians safe. He also asked what the South African government will do to stop bad groups. One group is called Operation Dudula. They want all foreigners out of South Africa. Chaponda said that many South Africans do business in Malawi and they are safe here. He said Malawi needs to bring home about six hundred of its people from South Africa.

The South African diplomat said her country is trying to help. She said they will keep repatriation free and that people can choose to stay or go. Many African countries are now worried about their people in South Africa. Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, Zimbabwe and Lesotho all said their people should be careful there.

ny.title: Anthu asanu ndi atatu akufa pakutulutsa bus ku South Africa
ny.body: Anthu asanu ndi atatu akufa pa mseche wa maseche ku South Africa pa Lachinayi. Bus yoyambira ku Malawi pa Lachiwiri yituluka pa laini ya N1 mu Limpopo Province. Bus idakutema pansi pafupi ndi Witvlag. Anthu makumi ana amadwala. Nthawi yomweyo, minisitala wa Malawi anakumana ndi wamkulu wa South Africa ku Lilongwe kuti akambe ayo Amalawi.
ny.body_more:
Bus yomwe idatema pansi inali ya Munorurama. Munthu umodzi akula kwambiri. Anthu makumi awiri ndi awiri amadwala kwambiri. Anthu khumi ndi zisanu amadwala pang'ono. Boma likuyang'anira kuti lione mayina a anthu onse omwe anali m'busino. Amalawi omwe ali ku South Africa akupatsidwa thandizo komanso matupi akubweledwa nyumbakuti.

Maseche uno si oyamba okha. Bus ina yobwereza kuchoka ku South Africa idatema pansi ku Zimbabwe pa Lachiwiri. M'maseche umenewo anthu khumi ndi zisanu adaphedwa ndipo anthu makumi mana ndi asanu amadwala. Anthu omwe adapulumuka abweledwa ku Malawi. Boma likubweleza matupi akupita ku Zimbabwe.

Minisitala wa Malawi George Chaponda anakumana ndi minisitala wa South Africa Mavivi Myakayaka-Manzini pa Lachisanu. Anayenera South Africa iti isamale amalawi. Anafunsefunsa kuti gulu limodzi litha kutani. Gulu linalitchedwa Operation Dudula. Ligonvera anthu onse a mmzinda ena kuti achioke. Chaponda anatero kuti anthu ambiri a South Africa amakagula zamatandalika ku Malawi ndipo ali otetezeka. Anatero kuti Malawi ikubwela anthu pafupifupi zikwi ndi zisanu kuchoka ku South Africa.

Mminisitala wa South Africa anatero kuti dziko lake likugwiritsa ntchito mphamvu. Anatero kuti anthu amazemba nokha kupita kuno kapena kudzakala. Maiko ina a Africa ayambitsa nkhawa za anthu awo mu South Africa. Ghana, Nigeria, Kenya, Zimbabwe ndi Lesotho onse anatero kuti anthu awo ayenera kusamala.
